    { "id": "d1", "term": "Artificial Intelligence (AI)", "verbatim_text": "The term “artificial intelligence” has the meaning provided in Section 238(g) of the John S.     { "id": "d2", "term": "AI Maturity", "verbatim_text": "The term “AI maturity” refers to a Federal Government organization’s capacity to successfully and responsibly adopt AI into their operations and decision-making across the organization, manage its risks, and comply with relevant Federal law, regulation, and policy on AI.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2024-03-28", "grounding_span": "The term “AI maturity” refers to a Federal Government organization’s capacity to", "location": "Section 6" },
    { "id": "d3", "term": "Safety-Impacting AI", "verbatim_text": "The term “safety-impacting AI” refers to AI whose output produces an action or serves as a principal basis for a decision that has the potential to significantly impact the safety of: Human life or well-being; Climate or environment; Critical infrastructure; or Strategic assets or resources.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2024-03-28", "grounding_span": "The term “safety-impacting AI” refers to AI whose output produces an", "location": "Section 6" },
    { "id": "d4", "term": "Rights-Impacting AI", "verbatim_text": "The term “rights-impacting AI” refers to AI whose output serves as a principal basis for a decision or action concerning a specific individual or entity that has a legal, material, binding, or similarly significant effect on that individual’s or entity’s: civil rights, civil liberties, or privacy; equal opportunities; or access to critical government resources or services.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2024-03-28", "grounding_span": "The term “rights-impacting AI” refers to AI whose output serves as a", "location": "Section 6" },
    { "id": "d5", "term": "Risks from the Use of AI", "verbatim_text": "The term “risks from the use of AI” refers to risks related to efficacy, safety, equity, fairness, transparency, accountability, appropriateness, or lawfulness of a decision or action resulting from the use of AI to inform, influence, decide, or execute that decision or action.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2024-03-28", "grounding_span": "The term “risks from the use of AI” refers to risks related to efficacy,", "location": "Section 6" },
    { "id": "d6", "term": "Significant Modification", "verbatim_text": "The term “significant modification” refers to an update to an AI application or to the conditions or context in which it is used that meaningfully alters the AI’s impact on rights or safety, such as through changing its functionality, underlying structure, or performance such that prior evaluations, training, or documentation become misleading.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2024-03-28", "grounding_span": "The term “significant modification” refers to an update to an AI", "location": "Section 6" },
    { "id": "d7", "term": "Automation Bias", "verbatim_text": "The term “automation bias” refers to the propensity for humans to inordinately favor suggestions from automated decision-making systems and to ignore or fail to seek out contradictory information made without automation.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2024-03-28", "grounding_span": "The term “automation bias” refers to the propensity for humans to inordinately", "location": "Section 6" },
    { "id": "d8", "term": "Applied Research", "verbatim_text": "The term “applied research” refers to original investigation undertaken in order to acquire new knowledge to determine the means by which a specific practical aim or objective may be met.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2024-03-28", "grounding_span": "The term “applied research” refers to original investigation undertaken in", "location": "Section 6" },

  "claims": [

    { "id": "cl7", "claim_text": "Agencies must review each current or planned use of AI to assess whether it is safety-impacting or rights-impacting.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "Agencies must review each current or planned use of AI to assess whether it matches", "location": "Section 5(b)" },
    { "id": "cl8", "claim_text": "Agencies must notify individuals when use of rights-impacting AI results in an adverse decision or action specifically concerning them.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "agencies must notify individuals when use of the AI", "location": "Section 5(c)(v)(D)" },
    { "id": "cl9", "claim_text": "Agencies must provide and maintain a mechanism for individuals to conveniently opt out of AI functionality in favor of a human alternative.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "maintain a mechanism for individuals to conveniently opt-out from the AI", "location": "Section 5(c)(v)(F)" },
    { "id": "cl10", "claim_text": "Artificial intelligence is one of the most powerful technologies of our time.", "claim_type": "speculative", "grounding_span": "Artificial intelligence (AI) is one of the most powerful technologies of our time", "location": "Section 1" },
